events.title: Interactive Mixology Class for 4 – 20 Guests
events.description: Learn to mix, shake, and enjoy your own handcrafted, seaso nally inspired cocktails! Upon arrival, we will greet you with a glass of sparkling rosé or brut. Sit back and sip while your instructor explains the bartender's tools of the trade and how to use them. Then, get your cameraphone ready, as you will be expertly guided through two shaken-or-stirred recipes to ensure a delicious final product. Perfect for: \* Bachelor/ Bachelorette Parties \* Birthday & Anniversary Celebrations \* Honeymoons \* Team-Building Events and Corporate Retreats Choose your theme! \* Seasonally-Inspired/Holidays \* Farm-to-Table Margaritas or Smashes \* Old Fashioned versus Manhattan \* Sparkling Wine Cocktails \* New Orleans Favorites \* NA and Mocktail Classes Must be 21 to enter Old Town Cellars. No guests under 21 allowed....
events.location: Old Town Cellars

events.title: Moose on the Loose Kid #2
events.description: July 18 - Round Valley North - Race #2 of the Moose on the Loose Series Join us for Moose on the Loose 2026, a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are June 13, July 18, August 15, and September 12, with all races beginning at 9:00 AM. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: I Dreamed I Saw Joe Hill lecture given by Sandy Brumley and Emmett Murray
events.description: Park City Museum and Friends of Ski Mountain Mining History will host a lecture called I Dreamed I Saw Joe Hill given by Sandy Brumley and Emmett Murray on Wednesday, June 10, 2026 from 5-6 p.m. held at the Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive. The lecture is in relation to a new exhibit called Park City Loves a Parade: Our Parades, Processions, and Protests from Past to Present which will be on display at the Park City Museum until April 2027. More information can be found at www.parkcityhistory.org. Baby boomers may recall a song about Joe Hill from 1970 The Woodstock Soundtrack Album. According to that song, Joe Hill never died but lives on “where working folks defend their rights.” In December of 2025, Joe Hill “lived on” in Park City through the Park City Professional Ski Patrol Association \(PCPSA\) as they successfully went on strike against Vail Resorts. Folks may not know that Joe Hill worked for The Silver King Coalition Mines in the days before the events that led up to his death by firing squad in the Utah State Prison in Sugarhouse. Emmet Murray of the PCPSA will provide some happy details from the ski patrol strike while Sandy Brumley, local PC history raconteur, will provide the tragic history of Joe Hill. A mountain host and hiking guide at Park City Mountain Resort, Sandy Brumley is a descendent of iron miners in Minnesota and an avid student of Park City History. This will be the fifth in a series of presentations he has made for the Museum. He is a hike leader for the Museum and a member of Friends of Ski Mountain Mining History. Emmett Murray is a ski patroller at the Canyons Resort at Park City Mountain and has just completed his ninth year. He was also the Vice President of PCPSPA during the strike which started on December 27, 2024 and lasted thirteen days....
events.location: Education and Collections Center, 2079 Sidewinder Dr, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Park City Film Presents: "American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez"
events.description: "American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez" Screens Thursday Jun. 11 at 7:00 PM An auteur emerges from America's underclass: from migrant farmworker to revolutionary artist, Luis Valdez changed American culture. In the 1960s, his El Teatro Campesino performed on flatbed trucks and helped mobilize workers to win the first farmworker union contract. His "Zoot Suit" was the first Chicano play on Broadway. Despite critical rejection that killed the show, he persevered, creating "La Bamba"-a breakout blockbuster that authentically depicted Mexican-American life to the world. Now in its 60th year, El Teatro continues to be a beacon for Latino/a creators. This is the story of an artist who gave voice to the overlooked and opened pathways for generations. Winner of the 2026 Sundance Film Festival Audience Award for U.S. Documentary and Audience Award for Festival Favorite. Presented as part of Latino Arts Festival. In Spanish and English with English subtitles. Admission is free with registration. Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...
events.location: Jim Santy Auditorium, 1255 Park Avenue, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: found-object collage/sculpture making
events.description: Thursday, June 11th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn skills for creating mixed-media 3-dimensional collage art pieces and be provided with a wide variety of reclaimed materials for use. Attendees will gain insight about artistic practices ranging from paper and scrapbooking materials to dry/drawing media and receive guidance on skills to assemble a sculpture. This class is great for anyone looking to expand their artistic practice through repurposed art supplies, examine their relationship to consumption and waste, or just have fun and try something new, whether they're a beginner or a pro in their creative endeavors! Anna, with Secondhand Salamander, is an artist and graduate student studying Sustainability whose lifelong practice has centered on the creative reuse of secondhand materials. Her multi-media photography and sculptural work have been exhibited by the Getty and the Utah Museum of Contemporary Art, as well as at local shows and markets. She dabbles in a bit of everything, doing live performance art and typewriter poetry pop-ups as "Anna Does Almost Anything." As a board member of Secondhand Salamander, SLC's creative reuse center, Anna teaches a variety of workshops on giving everyday materials new life in imaginative ways. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Jack Johnson: SURFILMUSIC
events.description: "SURFILMUSIC" Screens Friday Jun. 12 - Saturday Jun. 13 at 7:00 PM & Sunday Jun. 13 at 6:00 PM SURFILMUSIC, a documentary chronicling Johnson's evolution from surfer to filmmaker to world-renowned musician, weaves through the making of his iconic surf films "Thicker Than Water" \(1999\) and "The September Sessions" \(2000\). These classic films revealed Johnson's artistic point of view through his lens and guitar, ultimately launching his music career. The new film celebrates the lifelong friendships and ocean-driven community that shaped Johnson's path, and features many of the surfers who appeared in the original films, including Kelly Slater, Rob Machado, and the Malloy Brothers. It also captures the shared spirit of exploration and creativity that continues to inspire his music and environmentalism today. Beer and wine available for purchase. Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...
events.location: Jim Santy Auditorium, 1255 Park Avenue, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Kirtan in Park City | Music & Mantras by Spirit Soul
events.description: A joyful immersion in music, mantras, and dance Let Your Heart Soar Kirtan is a joyous form of meditation that combines music with hearing and singing sacred mantras. Singing these powerful transcendental sounds together frees our mind of stress and anxiety and awakens a deep sense of peace, connection, and joy. The Kirtan Experience is the work of a growing collective of meditators, musicians, artists and friends from around the world who have been deeply touched by this practice and feel inspired to share it with others. These events are an offering from our hearts, and we keep the events free so that anyone can attend. Our goal is to rekindle joy, peace and true inner freedom in a world deeply in need of healing. What You'll Experience • Live, immersive musical meditation with vibrant visuals, guided by lifelong kirtan artists and musicians. • Heartfelt singing of simple, powerful mantras that are easy to follow. No experience needed. • A comfortable, welcoming space where you can sing, dance, or simply kick back and listen. • A dynamic experience that flows between meditative moments and uplifting, energetic peaks. • A chance to unplug, reset, and feel alive. We welcome you to join us. Meditation Meets Celebration This is not a performance. It’s an enlivening experience created and shared by everyone who attends. A Living Tradition The gift we are sharing has been passed down with great care for over 500 years. Lovingly preserved in the lineage of Sri Krishna Chaitanya, the yoga sound is as spiritually potent and purifying today as it was centuries ago. Everyone Is Welcome Whether you're arriving with a light heart or a heavy burden, you are welcome here. In Kirtan, people come together across differences. It requires no agreement, belief, or explanation. Just come as you are and let the transcendental sound carry you into another world. Spring 2026 Tour This spring, Spirit Soul is bringing The Kirtan Experience to: • Lake Tahoe, NV • Sacramento, CA • Nevada ...
events.location: Park City Community Church (United Methodist), Park City, UT

events.title: Community Trail Run! Speedgoat Training!
events.description: **Speedgoat Training Run + Demo** Getting ready for Speedgoat? Join us for a community training run at the Armstrong Trailhead featuring demo shoes from HOKA, foot care support from Sidas, and recovery and performance services from FIKA Wellness. We'll be kicking off the run at **8:00 AM**, but arrive as early as **7:30 AM** to get set up with a pair of **HOKA Speedgoat 7 demo shoes**, connect with the team from **Sidas**for foot care solutions, and take advantage of pre-run services from **FIKA Wellness**. **We will be gathering in the First Time Lift parking lot at Park City Mountain Resort!** What to Expect 👟 **HOKA Speedgoat 7 Demo** Test the latest Speedgoat 7 on the trails Demo shoes available before the run 🦶 **Sidas Foot Care** Expert foot care solutions and fitting advice Learn how the right support can keep your feet comfortable and happy through long training days 💉 **FIKA Wellness** Free B12 shots Discounted NAD+ shots Pre-run mini IVs available Post-run recovery IVs available The Run This will be a self-guided community run with a suggested route and plenty of flexibility to choose your own adventure. Our recommended route follows **Armstrong Trail to Pinecone Ridge**, totaling approximately **18 miles** with plenty of climbing—an excellent training day for runners preparing for Speedgoat. Looking for fewer miles? No problem. There are several shorter route options from the trailhead, making this event accessible to a wide range of runners and training goals. Who Should Come? Whether you're training for Speedgoat, building fitness for another race, or simply looking for a great morning on the trails with the community, we'd love to have you join us. All paces are welcome!...
events.location: First Time Lift, Park City, UT

events.description: "SURFILMUSIC" Screens Friday Jun. 12 - Saturday Jun. 13 at 7:00 PM & Sunday Jun. 14 at 6:00 PM SURFILMUSIC, a documentary chronicling Johnson's evolution from surfer to filmmaker to world-renowned musician, weaves through the making of his iconic surf films "Thicker Than Water" \(1999\) and "The September Sessions" \(2000\). These classic films revealed Johnson's artistic point of view through his lens and guitar, ultimately launching his music career. The new film celebrates the lifelong friendships and ocean-driven community that shaped Johnson's path, and features many of the surfers who appeared in the original films, including Kelly Slater, Rob Machado, and the Malloy Brothers. It also captures the shared spirit of exploration and creativity that continues to inspire his music and environmentalism today. Beer and wine available for purchase. Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: The Great Weed Roundup with Summit Land Conservancy
events.description: Summit Land Conservancy and Summit County are looking for motivated volunteers to help us uproot noxious weeds! With spring fully in swing, join us for our next Stewardship Saturday at Miss Billie’s Preserve (Summit Community Gardens)! We will be pulling invasive dyer's woad, thistle, and white top while they are in bloom and before they can release their seeds. These are all aggressive, introduced plants that grow rapidly and create dense thickets which crowd out our native grasses and wildflowers. When we remove these weeds, we help to restore our local grassland ecosystem and ensure that native plants can thrive and support the wildlife that depends on them.This project will require using gardening tools and manual pulling to stop the spread of these weeds in their tracks. Participants should bring clothing suitable for a full day outside (long pants, long sleeves, sun protection, warm layers, hat, sunglasses, and sturdy shoes), gardening gloves if you have them, along with a water bottle and snacks. Summit Land Conservancy will bring coffee, bagels and cold refreshments to keep you caffeinated, fueled and hydrated while you work!...
events.location: Miss Billie's Preserve, 4056 Shadow Mountain Drive, Park City, UT 84098, USA

Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are **June 13**, **July 18**, **August 15**, and **September 12**, with all races beginning at **9:00 AM**. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....

events.title: Kirtan in Park City | Music & Mantras by Spirit Soul
events.description: A joyful immersion in music, mantras, and dance Let Your Heart Soar Kirtan is a joyous form of meditation that combines music with hearing and singing sacred mantras. Singing these powerful transcendental sounds together frees our mind of stress and anxiety and awakens a deep sense of peace, connection, and joy. The Kirtan Experience is the work of a growing collective of meditators, musicians, artists and friends from around the world who have been deeply touched by this practice and feel inspired to share it with others. These events are an offering from our hearts, and we keep the events free so that anyone can attend. Our goal is to rekindle joy, peace and true inner freedom in a world deeply in need of healing. What You'll Experience • Live, immersive musical meditation with vibrant visuals, guided by lifelong kirtan artists and musicians. • Heartfelt singing of simple, powerful mantras that are easy to follow. No experience needed. • A comfortable, welcoming space where you can sing, dance, or simply kick back and listen. • A dynamic experience that flows between meditative moments and uplifting, energetic peaks. • A chance to unplug, reset, and feel alive. We welcome you to join us. Meditation Meets Celebration This is not a performance. It’s an enlivening experience created and shared by everyone who attends. A Living Tradition The gift we are sharing has been passed down with great care for over 500 years. Lovingly preserved in the lineage of Sri Krishna Chaitanya, the yoga sound is as spiritually potent and purifying today as it was centuries ago. Everyone Is Welcome Whether you're arriving with a light heart or a heavy burden, you are welcome here. In Kirtan, people come together across differences. It requires no agreement, belief, or explanation. Just come as you are and let the transcendental sound carry you into another world. Spring 2026 Tour This spring, Spirit Soul is bringing The Kirtan Experience to: • Lake Tahoe, NV • Sacramento, CA • Nevada ...

events.title: An Evening with Dr. N. T. Wright
events.description: 2000 years of questions — 1 night of answers. The questions on the table are the ones keeping thoughtful leaders up at 2 am — a groaning creation, a divided church, and a world that’s lost its patience for easy answers. They’re the ones surfacing in your sermons, your elder meetings, and your quiet moments of doubt. Dr. N.T. Wright has spent a lifetime carefully working through difficult questions about faith. Join us for an evening of exploring difficult questions together. 5:00 – Appetizers & Mingling 5:30 – Presentation by N.T. Wright 6:30 – Q&A with N.T. Wright 7:00 – Continued Conversations \(optional\) $15 secures your seat. RSVP is required. events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Volunteer Dig Day June 16th
events.description: Thanks for your interest in volunteering to help us build trails! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. Mountain Trails Foundation will be completing two new sections of directional, downhill flow trail on PCMR. To accomplish this, we will be utilizing volunteers to complete some of the finish work. Work anticipated will be the removal of debris from trail, smoothing of tread, compaction of features, and the removal of vegetation from the trail corridor. These events are special, as they are in the evening and generally end around sunset and, as everyone knows, that is a wonderful time to be on the mountain. We'll meet at the First Time Parking Lot \(https://maps.app.goo.gl/SE1bcWNVw4sH17vZA\) at 5:00pm, and shuttles to the worksite will leave at 5:15 sharp! We'll return to the parking lot around 8pm. We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. Minors must be accompanied by a guardian, and we ask for a 5:1 ratio of minors to adults. Timeframe: 5pm Meet at the trailhead 5:15pm Shuttles leave for worksite 8:00 Return to parking lot Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!

events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description: A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level. Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath. \*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged \* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing. Some benefits of breathwork include: - Connect to your creativity - Increase Self Love and appreciation - Stress relief - Increased Self Awareness - Ease symptoms of Depression - Ease symptoms of Anxiety - Peace of mind - Open the heart - Increased Intuition - Relaxation - Release trauma and fear - Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings 10-Class Pass Rate: $11 Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: Tibet: The Roof of the World Presentation
events.description: 5:00pm on Wednesday, 17 June 2026 Community Room @ 1255 Park Avenue Americans can only visit Tibet as part of a pre-booked guided tour, but you can learn about this fascinating country in East Asia at the Park City Library. Experienced lecturer and new Parkite Deborah Jennings will give a lecture and a slideshow, then will take questions from the audience. She'll touch on geography, politics, religion, and culture - including yaks and butter, Buddhism and the Dalai Lama, and Mt. Everest. events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.title: Park City Community Foundation's Summer Solstice Celebration
events.description: Join Park City Community Foundation as we celebrate summer and show support for our local community! Start your weekend with drinks, food, live music, face painting, games, local resources for parents & kids, and fun for all ages! Bring your friends and get connected at this free, community-wide celebration and fair. Be sure to stop by our tables to learn about Park City Community Foundation and our initiatives including Early Childhood Alliance, Youth United, Zero Food Waste, Women's Giving Fund, and Community Pass Club - and how you can contribute and get involved. You will also have a chance to bid on Cabriolet Cabins donated by CVMA and Vail Resorts Epic Promise Foundation and Park City Mountain, with funds supporting Park City Community Foundation. Parking is available in the upper village lots. Thank you **Vail Resorts Epic Promise Foundation and Park City Mountain** for sponsoring this event....

events.title: Recess on Main - A Wine Importer's Spanish Faves
events.description: Bon Vivant Imports owner Brad Jensen will serve tastes of his favorite, under $45 wines from Spain. Most of the Spanish wineries he imports from are organic, small producers run by families for 100s of years. His stories of tasting vintages with the winemakers, picking grapes with the grandkids, and learning the reverence with which they approach their wines are fun and enlightening. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. Don't we all deserve a little recess time?...

events.description: A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level. Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath. \*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged \* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing. Some benefits of breathwork include: - Connect to your creativity - Increase Self Love and appreciation - Stress relief - Increased Self Awareness - Ease symptoms of Depression - Ease symptoms of Anxiety - Peace of mind - Open the heart - Increased Intuition - Relaxation - Release trauma and fear - Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings 10-Class Pass Rate: $11 Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.title: Thriving Land Thursdays: Weed Wrangling on the 910 Ranch
events.description: Join us at the 910 Ranch for a hands-on day of stewardship and community connection! We'll work together to restore the riparian corridor by removing noxious and invasive weeds that crowd out native species and degrade wildlife habitat. What to Bring & Wear: Wear long pants, long sleeves, and sturdy closed-toe shoes or boots. Pack sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen), water and snacks, and layers in case of changing weather. Please bring sunglasses to act as protective eyewear! Participants may want bring their own thick, sturdy gloves and clippers. Summit County will have equipment available to borrow. Safety Notice: Participation in weed removal can be hazardous. Hazards may include cuts, punctures, scrapes, tripping, heavy lifting, sharp edges, and exposure to rusted metal or other environmental risks. By signing up and attending this activity, you acknowledge these risks, accept full responsibility for your participation, and agree to the terms of the waiver. Your safety is our priority—please work carefully, follow instructions, and look out for one another. This is a great opportunity to roll up your sleeves, meet like-minded people, and directly contribute to preserving and enhancing one of our community’s treasured open spaces....
events.location: 910 Ranch, Park City, UT

events.title: nature relief prints
events.description: nature relief prints Thursday, June 25th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required Join us for a hands-on nature relief printing workshop where participants will create bold, large-scale prints using a tree stump as the printing surface. Each student will produce a striking tree print \(approximately 25 x 40 inches\) and explore additional smaller prints using plants and flowers. This workshop introduces the fundamentals of relief printing, including inking techniques, proper ink coverage, and printmaking processes. Participants will gain experience working with natural textures and forms while creating one-of-a-kind prints to take home. Please wear clothing that can get dirty, as printing ink may stain and bring any plants from home you want to print. Rebecca Pletsch is a Utah-based painter and mixed media artist whose work explores themes of memory, impermanence, and perception through painting and collage. With over twenty years of experience, her practice merges traditional techniques with experimental processes, creating layered works that evoke atmosphere, fragmentation, and emotional resonance. Her work has received numerous awards and honors and has been exhibited in major museums and galleries throughout Utah, including the Utah Museum of Contemporary Art, Ogden Contemporary Arts, the Kimball Art Center, the Rio Gallery, and the Springville Museum of Art. In addition to her studio practice, she is deeply engaged in arts education and is passionate about helping others explore and develop their creative voice. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: Park City Film Presents: NATCHEZ
events.description: "Natchez" captures an unsettling clash between history and memory in a small Mississippi town; a layered mosaic of people contending with the weight of the past in a place where it is always present. Equal parts amusing and disturbing, we journey through an antebellum tourist destination at a crossroads as it grapples with a deeply troubled history that is so thoroughly ingrained in its present, we're left to wonder if it's actually past at all. "The genteel politeness on display at the start of the film falls away, revealing an unsettling core" -HOLLYWOOD REPORTER Post-film Q&A with director Suzannah Herbert. Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) - a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. More information at http://parkcityfilm.org...
events.location: Jim Santy Auditorium, 1255 Park Avenue, Park City, UT 84060, USA

With short lectures and demonstrations all on location, develop your own visual language and body of work. Participants should bring their own supplies and have prior painting experience. This workshop is designed for artists ready to deepen their relationship with landscape and push their work beyond observation into expression....

Expect bright acidity, mineral-driven flavors, and refreshing styles perfect for summer sipping. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. Don't we all deserve a little recess time?...

events.title: Cory Mon
events.description: ..music, my blessing, my curse, its in me and it must come out i can't help it, i must play, write, compose and perform. Without i am incomplete, while with call me misfit in this world of the incorporated. I am a man confused and divided thus in recourse i am often brightened and enlightened. I emphatically give way to both light and dark, i live in pleasure and pain. my name: cory. Singer/songwriter Cory Mon has a distinctly unique, albeit pleasant voice in the world of music. “Happy \(sometimes sad\), gritty, funky, folksy, bluesy, rocky music… I guess”… he says when asked the dreaded, “So what type of music do you play?” Assuming the life of a musician, Cory has thrived as well as endured through the many ups and downs that have come throughout his well tenured 20\+ year music career. Cory has released 60\+ original songs and toured with and/or supported a handful of his favorite artists: JJ Grey & Mofro, Sean Hayes, The Avett Brothers, Patterson Hood etc. www.corymon.com...

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Remaining Native
events.description: Ku Stevens \(17\) is the solo runner at his high school with no coach. Living on the Yerington Paiute reservation in Northwest Nevada he needs more to be seen by his dream school, the University of Oregon. As Ku trains, unreconciled emotions unearth the memory of his great-grandfather, Frank Quinn. At 8 years old, Frank ran 50 miles across the desert to escape an Indian boarding school. Frank's story becomes interwoven withKu's journey to run a collegiate qualifying time. Will Ku outrun his history or will he learn to run in parallel with it to achieve his dreams? Post-film Q&A with director Paige Bethmann. Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) - a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. Tickets and more information at parkcityfilm.org http://parkcityfilm.org/...

Then, gather around our signature eight-foot paella pan, featuring squid ink rice with lobster, razor clams, octopus, and shrimp, as Deer Valley’s culinary team prepares a three-course dinner before your eyes. events.title: Third Act
events.description: Generations of artists call Robert A. Nakamura “The Godfather of Asian American film,” but his son, Tad, calls him Dad. As the filmmaking son of a filmmaking legend, Tad uses the lessons his dad taught him to decipher the legacy of an aging man who was a child survivor of the WWII incarceration of Japanese Americans, a successful photographer who gave it up to tell his own story, an activist at the dawn of a social movement—and a father whose struggles have won his son freedoms that eluded Japanese Americans of his generation. As Parkinson’s Disease clouds his memory, Tad sets out to retrieve his story—and in the process discovers his own. The two have made films together, with Robert always by Tad’s side. “Third Act” is most likely the last. 2025 Sundance Film Festival premiere. Post-Film Q&A with director Tadashi Nakamura and others TBA. “A richly layered examination of the generational effects of segregation, grief, parenthood, and art’s ability to help us make sense of it all.” — Courtney Small, POV Magazine Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) – a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence...
events.location: Park City Film, Park City, UT

events.title: Give Me the Ball
events.description: World champion tennis trailblazer Billie Jean King has had a game-changing impact on culture and sports. Rare archive and candid interviews with Billie Jean and those closest to her reveal how one woman put changing the world ahead of saving herself. The story of professional women’s sports could not be told without Billie Jean King. Her status is so iconic, some might lose sight of the real human being — her struggles, her flaws, and her all-consuming, competitive drive. In fighting for equity for herself and others, King was uncompromising, to the point of subordinating her personal well-being by hiding her sexual orientation and eating disorders. Directors Liz Garbus and Elizabeth Wolff present Billie Jean King in full, in her own words, and with archival footage that brings the dark times and indelible victories to life again. With refreshing honesty and the hard-won perspective of having lived through it all, “Give Me the Ball!” is an electric portrait of one of the greatest of all time. 2026 Sundance Film Festival premiere. Post-film panel discussion TBA. Presented with Summit Pride Foundation. Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) – a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence...
events.location: Park City Film, Park City, UT

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....
events.location: Glenwood Cemetery, Park City, UT

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: The Pendry Plaza
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. events.title: Jylian Gustlin + Lola: Exhibition
events.description: Gallery MAR is pleased to present a summer exhibition featuring new work by Lola and Jylian Gustlin, opening **July 3rd** with an artist reception from **6–9 pm** at the gallery’s Main Street location in Park City. On view for **three weeks**, the exhibition brings together two distinctive contemporary artists whose practices explore abstraction through color, material, and perception. Lola, a self-taught visual artist based in the San Francisco Bay Area, is best known for her intensely vibrant abstractions created with tinted epoxy resin. Working on a raised horizontal surface, she allows the resin to flow, pool, and spill over the edges of the panel, creating luminous compositions framed by gravity-defying drips that have become a signature of her work. Her newest series, *Quiet Places*, marks an evolution in her practice—moving away from densely layered forms toward simpler shapes, softer palettes, and shadowy overlays that invite viewers to experience moments of calm and serenity. For Lola, color remains the central language of emotion, and each work is intended to create an experience that allows viewers to find their own meaning and sense of optimism within the composition. In conversation with Lola’s emotive abstractions are the richly layered paintings of Jylian Gustlin. Influenced by the San Francisco Bay Area Figurative painters and informed by her background in computer science and digital graphics programming, Gustlin’s work explores the intersection of art, mathematics, and perception. Drawing on concepts such as the Fibonacci sequence and entropy, she creates complex compositions using oil, acrylic, charcoal, wax, gold leaf, pastel, and graphite. Gustlin builds her surfaces through drawing, painting, and scratching into the material itself, resulting in paintings that feel almost sculptural—dynamic fields of gesture, symbol, and color that encourage viewers to look closely and discover new relationships within the work. Together, Lola and Gustlin...
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.description: A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level. Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. Some benefits of breathwork include: - Connect to your creativity - Increase Self Love and appreciation - Stress relief - Increased Self Awareness - Ease symptoms of Depression - Ease symptoms of Anxiety - Peace of mind - Open the heart - Increased Intuition - Relaxation - Release trauma and fear - Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings 10-Class Pass Rate: $11 Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.description: On July 8, Opera on the Patio pairs the intimate, emotional ly direct songs of Robert Schumann and Clara Schumann with the luminous, color-rich world of Claude Debussy. These composers changed how music feels and moves - and we invite you to explore their works with us! Back for its third season, Opera on the Patio is a free outdoor performance at the Park City Library. Bring a picnic, your family, and even your dog for an evening of beloved opera set against the backdrop of a summer sunset. Enjoy great music in a relaxed, welcoming setting \(no tickets required\). Check back soon for more details, including the concert program, performer biographies, and more!...

events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. events.title: Recess on Main - Wines for Day Drinking (aka: How to drink like a French person)
events.description: Join Professor **Olivier Peronnet** as your guide to sipping like the French do; elegantly and yes, often before noon! Explore food-friendly wines that pair perfectly with brunch, and long, lazy conversations. Add some baguettes and cheese and you're well into the afternoon before ya know it. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. Don't we all deserve a little recess time?...

events.title: Allen Stone
events.description: Grammy-nominated artist Allen Stone delivers an unforgettable fusion of soul, funk, and feel-good energy. Allen Stone, a soul/R&B luminary, has enraptured global audiences with his electrifying performances and soulful melodies, embodying the essence of classic soul legends. His genre-defying style, blending funk, gospel, folk-rock, and soul, has garnered critical acclaim and a devoted following. Stone's journey to musical stardom commenced in Chewelah, Washington, where his upbringing immersed him in gospel music as a pastor's son. Relocating to Seattle, he tirelessly toured the West Coast in his '87 Buick, swiftly gaining acclaim for his dynamic live shows, often playing up to 200 dates annually alongside luminaries such as Stevie Wonder, Al Green, Dave Matthews Band, Erykah Badu, and Gary Clark Jr. Notably, Stone recently hit the road with Chris Stapleton, further solidifying his status as a tour de force in the music industry. Alongside his prolific touring schedule, Stone has graced national television screens and mentored on ABC's American Idol, underscoring his esteemed position in the music realm. Stone continues to garner critical acclaim with his latest body of work, Mystery, featuring the title track in collaboration with PJ Morton. This release brings infectious rhythms and emotional depth, solidifying Stone’s place as a powerful voice in modern soul music. Opening support Wyatt Pike is an indie-folk songwriter known for crafting story-driven songs filled with vivid imagery, emotional honesty, and reflections on real-life experiences. He first gained attention with his debut EP, Brand New Boy, and most recently released the acoustic project Face The Weather. His music explores the journey of personal growth and self-discovery. As Alt Recess noted, his work represents "the next chapter—more assured, more grounded, but still brimming with the tender honesty that put him on the map." His upcoming 2026 releases will continue to build on that authentic and

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Chef Sarah Glover Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 11 and 12, Chef Sarah Glover brings her signature, land-driven approach to the flame, preparing locally sourced game, fresh-baked breads, and seasonal ingredients over open fire. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community gatherings, and a Native Fashion Show. Sponsors and partners are invited to help elevate Native voices, support Native artists and entrepreneurs, and align their brand with a meaningful cultural experience rooted in respect, representation, and celebration. Event Schedule Friday, July 10, 2026 * Native Market 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Sundial Pavilion * Hoop Dance Performances 11:00 AM–5:00 PM Forum Stage, Canyons Village Saturday, July 11, 2026 * Native Market 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Canyons Village * Hoop Dance Performances 10:00 AM–5:00 PM Forum Stage, Canyons Village * Native Fashion Show 4:00 PM–8:00 PM Kokopelli Grand Ballroom...

events.location: Snow Park Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Sarah Glover Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 11 and 12, Chef Sarah Glover brings her signature, land-driven approach to the flame, preparing locally sourced game, fresh-baked breads, and seasonal ingredients over open fire. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!
events.location: Old Town Transit Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Glenwood Cemetery Tours
events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....

Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. events.description: Park City Museum will be giving tours of the Historic Glenwood Cemetery located at 401 Silver King Drive on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m. from June 30th to September 17th. We have also added three Saturday dates this year including July 25th, August 22nd, and September 5th from 10:30 a.m. to 11:45 a.m., and a few Monday evening tours including June 29th, July 6th, July 20th, August 10th, August 24th, and September 14th all starting at 5:30 p.m. Discover the history of the Cemetery and those residents who are buried there. Please register for a tour at www.parkcityhistory.org. Tours will be $15 per person. Please meet at the Glenwood Cemetery by the gate, bring a water bottle, wear sunscreen and comfortable walking shoes. Parking will be on Silver King Drive near the Cemetery. If you have any questions, please contact Diane Knispel education@parkcityhistory.org or call 435-574-9554....

events.title: Raising Voices En Pointe - Free Screening + Q&A
events.description: RAISING VOICES EN POINTE - Free film screening \+ Q&A Ballet lovers hold deep reverence for classics like The Nutcracker and La Bayadère, and for decades many fans overlooked the racist Asian caricatures embedded in some of these productions. When ballet dancers Phil Chan \(resident choreographer at the Oakland Ballet Company\) and Georgina Pazcoguin \(first female AAPI soloist at the New York City Ballet and recent Ballet West guest artist\) launched the Final Bow for Yellow Face movement, they were met with pushback from traditionalists who accused them of trying to change history. Post-film discussion and reception with Ballet West Artistic Director Adam Sklute and Phil Chan, co-founder of Final Bow for Yellowface. Part of the Ballet West Summit Festival July 15-17...

Learn simple, go-to pairings for herbs, vegetables, and vibrant summer dishes. Taste herbaceous whites, high-acid whites, dry rosés, textured whites, and light-bodied reds. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. events.title: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — If there’s one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it’s Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the “Ode to Joy,” as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner—an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven’s Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season. July 17 | Gates Open: 6:30 p.m. | Show Time: 8 p.m. July 18 | Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.

events.title: Matilda & Gozney Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 18 and 19, Gozney and Matilda come together to serve up an unforgettable pizza experience. Gozney’s professional-grade ovens, engineered to hit blazing temperatures over 900°F with their signature rolling flame, set the stage for Matilda’s wood-fired Neapolitan pizzas, crafted with the same passion that’s made them a local favorite among both travelers and regulars. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

From the chart-topping smash “Ice Ice Baby” to the smooth harmonies of “I Wanna Sex You Up” and “All 4 Love,” this is the ultimate summer celebration of 90s nostalgia. Don’t miss one of the most fun nights of the summer with iconic artists, throwback vibes, and nonstop hits. events.title: Kenneth Peloke: Year of the Horse
events.description: Working in oil on large-format canvases, Kenneth Peloke approaches the horse not as subject but as a vessel for energy, instinct, and the kind of presence that resists easy translation into paint. His process is physical and deliberate, built through layers of mark-making that accumulate into something that feels less rendered than conjured. Year of the Horse brings together new works that explore what the animal has always represented in the human imagination: freedom, strength, the untamed thing we are drawn to and can never fully possess. Join us July 18th to experience new works by Kenneth Peloke at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, Park City, UT

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." events.title: Matilda & Gozney Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. On July 18 and 19, Gozney and Matilda come together to serve up an unforgettable pizza experience. Gozney’s professional-grade ovens, engineered to hit blazing temperatures over 900°F with their signature rolling flame, set the stage for Matilda’s wood-fired Neapolitan pizzas, crafted with the same passion that’s made them a local favorite among both travelers and regulars. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.description: Greetings Volunteers! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. We'll be meeting at the private parking area directly west of the Bonanza Flat parking lot at 8:30 am \(no fee to park\). We'll then head out to do dirt work on the new hiking trail connecting 9k and Mid Mountain. If you're a mountain biker wondering why you should be doing work on a hike-only trail, please remember that these options alleviate traffic and help to optimize bike-only options throughout the system. We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. This project presents an excellent opportunity to create a unique legacy trail that will benefit you and the community for years to come! Timeframe: 8:30-8:45 Meet at the trailhead 8:45 Head to worksite 12:00 Return to trailhead Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: ttps://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Mozart’s “Jupiter”
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart’s second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. Framing the concerto are Mozart’s two final symphonies—written just weeks apart—revealing the fascinating journey of the composer's musical maturation and his unmistakable brilliance. One of Mozart’s most recognizable works, his Symphony No. 40 uses a minor key—a rare choice for the composer—to build emotional intensity and dark lyricism. In contrast, the program concludes with the joyful Symphony No. 41, whose grandeur and musical tour de force ultimately earned it the nickname “Jupiter,” honoring the Roman god....

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....

events.description: Join us for an incredible night of good vibes and heavy grooves as we bring our unique style of high desert reggae, rock, and groove music to Heber City Market The Last Light is a reggae based groove collective composed of 7 artists who call the Southern Utah Desert home. They Released their Debut EP, "Music Is The Medicine" in 2023 and have performed shows in Utah, Nevada, California, & Florida in the last 4 years. events.title: 80s Weekend with 38 Special
events.description: 38 Special - Live in Concert Bring on the anthems. 38 Special delivers high-energy, Southern-fried rock that defined an era and still packs the house decades later. Known for blending catchy hooks with driving guitar riffs, the band has become a staple of American rock radio and live performance. With iconic hits like "Hold On Loosely," "Caught Up in You," and "Rockin' Into the Night," 38 Special has built a legacy of feel-good, sing-along songs that cross generations. Their live shows are tight, powerful, and packed with the kind of rock-and-roll energy that turns a concert into a shared memory. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this is a show made for fans of classic rock, summer nights, and timeless music that still hits just right....

events.title: Recess on Main - The Wines of Burgundy
events.description: Explore with our authentic (not an actor) French Professor, Olivier Peronnet, the beautiful vineyards of Burgundy, while sipping wines as you hear centuries-old stories about the area and hear about the vineyards his family grows. Discover how this iconic region's terroir, (aka growing region) transforms grapes into white and red poetry. Expect a little je ne sais quoi magic in every sip. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. Don't we all deserve a little recess time?...

events.title: Speedgoat Mountain Races by UTMB®
events.description: Embark on the adventure of a lifetime at the Speedgoat Mountain Races by UTMB®. Take on the trails winding throughout Snowbird in the 10k, 28k or 50k. Dig deep to unleash your inner grit and be rewarded with breathtaking panoramic views of Little Cottonwood Canyon. While here, enjoy the amenities of a world-renowned ski resort throughout race weekend, including lodging, a variety of dining options and post-race relaxation at The Cliff Spa. All races start and finish on the Plaza Deck at the Snowbird Center, Entry 2. The race village is located in the Upper Circle parking lot outside the Snowbird Center. Limited Tram rides will be offered for spectators on the morning of each race day. Lodging discounts are available for registered participants. Parking is available for participants, friends, family and spectators in Snowbird's main lots. Race Details: 10k \(6.2 Miles\) Date: Friday, July 24 Start Time: 9 am Elevation Gain: 1,640\+ ft Start/Finish: The Plaza Deck at The Snowbird Center 30k \(19.2 Miles\) Date: Friday, July 24 Start Time: 7:30 am Elevation Gain: 6,561\+ ft Start/Finish: The Plaza Deck at The Snowbird Center 50k \(31 Miles\) Date: Saturday, July 25 Start Time: 6:30 am Elevation Gain: 11,318\+ ft Start/Finish: The Plaza Deck at The Snowbird Center...
