events.title: Overland Cookery Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. Led by Overland Cookery’s expedition chefs, this weekend draws inspiration from cooking in remote environments. Open fire techniques and thoughtful preparation create an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, it is an easy, social setting to spend the afternoon, where food, fire, and the outdoors come together. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite & String Trio “Time for Three”
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite takes flight in this spectacular program led by Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner. First, GRAMMY®-winning trio Time for Three—known for their genre-defying blend of classical, Americana, and contemporary styles— takes the stage for Kevin Puts’ Contact, the winner of a GRAMMY® for Best Contemporary Classical Composition. Written specifically for the group by the Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, the work explores themes of human connection and highlights the trio’s dynamic, longtime partnership with the Utah Symphony. Then, hear one of the most thrilling pieces ever written echoing through the Wasatch Mountains as the orchestra takes you on a journey from the explosive “Infernal Dance” to the powerful finale of Stravinsky’s Firebird. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA
events.venue_name: Deer Valley Ski Resort Main Base

events.title: 80s Weekend with Taylor Dayne and Exposé
events.description: Taylor Dayne Taylor Dayne is an American pop icon. With a career spanning three decades, Taylor’s Groundbreaking debut single “Tell It To My Heart” turned her into an overnight international star in 1987. She followed the smash hit with seventeen Top 20 singles over the course of her three-decade career, including Number Ones “Love Will Lead You Back”, “Prove Your Love”, and “I’ll Always Love You.” Taylor has sold over 75 million albums and singles worldwide, earned three GRAMMY nominations, an American Music Award, multiple New York Music Awards, received New York Music Hall of Fame honors and ranked as the Number 18 Female Dance Artist of all time by Rolling Stone magazine. She is one of the very few artists in musical history to successfully crossover into almost every musical genre and chart with hit singles in Pop, Dance, R&B, Adult contemporary, and Rock. Exposé Get ready for a night of unforgettable 80s and 90s dance-pop when Exposé takes the stage. This legendary female vocal group from Miami helped define the freestyle and dance-pop sound of the late 1980s and early 1990s, delivering a string of iconic hits that still fill dance floors today. With powerhouse vocals and timeless melodies, Exposé became the first group ever to score four Top 10 hits from their debut album, including classics like “Come Go With Me,” “Point of No Return,” “Let Me Be the One,” and their No. 1 smash hit “Seasons Change.” Featuring the celebrated voices of Jeanette Jurado, Ann Curless, and Gioia Bruno, Exposé continues to tour and delight fans worldwide with their signature blend of high-energy dance anthems and heartfelt ballads. From chart-topping radio hits to beloved freestyle club favorites, Exposé delivers a show packed with nostalgia, powerful vocals, and infectious rhythms that will have audiences singing along all night. Join us for an electrifying evening celebrating one of the most successful girl groups of the 80s and a sound that defined a generation.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT
events.address: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Best Day Ever
events.description: “Best Day Ever” follows adaptive mountain bikers Greg Durso and Allie Bianchi as they tackle the daily challenges of disability-and find joy, connection, and belonging in Vermont’s mountain biking community. Set in the Green Mountains, this is a story of grit, independence, and what’s possible when community and creativity come together. At its heart is the world’s first fully adaptive trail network—built to prove that everyone belongs outside. Visually stunning and emotionally powerful, “Best Day Ever” challenges perceptions of disability and celebrates community. Post-film Q&A with Greg Durso. Reception to follow in Community Room. Presented as part of the Raising Voices Film Series in partnership with the Kelly Brush Foundation. Ticket proceeds benefit the Kelly Brush Foundation...

events.title: Volunteer Dig Day July 28th - Anchor Trees
events.description: Greetings Volunteers! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. We'll be meeting at the private parking area directly west of the Bonanza Flat parking lot at 5pm \(no fee to park\). We'll then head out to do dirt work on the new hiking trail connecting 9k and Mid Mountain. If you're a mountain biker wondering why you should be doing work on a hike-only trail, please remember that these options alleviate traffic and help to optimize bike-only options throughout the system. We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. This project presents an excellent opportunity to create a unique legacy trail that will benefit you and the community for years to come! Timeframe: 5:00pm Meet at the trailhead 5:15pm Head to worksite 8:00pm Return to trailhead Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: ttps://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...
events.location: Private Parking Lot - Bonanza Flat, Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.

events.title: Fred Tackett & Scott Sharrard (from Little Feat)
events.description: Fred Tackett and Scott Sharrard are stepping out from the electric swamp boogie of Little Feat and hitting the road with a couple acoustic guitars, along with some great songs and stories. They’ll be pulling material from Little Feat, their own songwriting catalogues, and their own history that include stints with everyone from Bob Dylan to The Gregg Allman Band. Fred Tackett is an American multi-instrumentalist and songwriter best known for his work with Little Feat. Hailing from Little Rock, AR, Tackett began his musical journey at a young age beginning with trumpet, drums, and guitar before expanding to mandolin, vocals, and other instruments. Before joining Little Feat, Tackett was a prolific session musician, contributing to recordings by artists such as Bob Dylan, John Lennon, Jimmy Webb, Rod Stewart, Bob Seger, Bonnie Raitt, Kenny Rogers, Jackson Browne, Boz Scaggs, Tom Waits, and countless others. His association with Little Feat dates back to the 1970s, when he collaborated as a sideman on several of their albums, including Dixie Chicken and Time Loves a Hero. He became an official member of the band in 1988 when they released the legendary album Let It Roll. Tackett's multifaceted musicianship and extensive career have solidified his reputation as a distinguished figure in the American music scene. American music courses through Scott Sharrard’s heart and soul. Since 2019, Sharrard has been the lead singer and guitarist for Little Feat, preserving the magic of the group’s beloved guiding light, the late Lowell George, and reimagining that energy with his own mojo. He’s recorded on their Grammy-Nominated album, Sam’s Place. He performed, wrote and co-produced songs for their 2025 album, Strike Up The Band. As a guitarist, he has shared the stage with Derek Trucks, Warren Haynes, Tommy Emmanuel, Robert Randolph, Charlie Starr, and Hubert Sumlin. Prior to Little Feat, Sharrard served as Gregg Allman’s music director, band leader and producer for man...

events.title: David Young & The Interstate Kings
events.description: David Young & The Interstate Kings are a three piece Roots-Rock group serving up vintage American music with . Formed in a small town in Maine in 2015, the band consists of David Young on guitar/vocals, Deven Young on drums, and Seth Martin on bass. After initial success in the Maine scene including appearances on Maine television staples such as 207 and The Nite Show, The Interstate Kings refocused their desire to bring their brand of bluesy, crunchy Americana to a wider audience and moved to Nashville, TN, where they're currently based.

Very helpful wine experience to figure out whether you like wines from warm or cool regions. Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. events.title: Fred Tackett & Scott Sharrard (from Little Feat)
events.description: Fred Tackett and Scott Sharrard are stepping out from the electric swamp boogie of Little Feat and hitting the road with a couple acoustic guitars, along with some great songs and stories. They’ll be pulling material from Little Feat, their own songwriting catalogues, and their own history that include stints with everyone from Bob Dylan to The Gregg Allman Band. Fred Tackett is an American multi-instrumentalist and songwriter best known for his work with Little Feat. Hailing from Little Rock, AR, Tackett began his musical journey at a young age beginning with trumpet, drums, and guitar before expanding to mandolin, vocals, and other instruments. Before joining Little Feat, Tackett was a prolific session musician, contributing to recordings by artists such as Bob Dylan, John Lennon, Jimmy Webb, Rod Stewart, Bob Seger, Bonnie Raitt, Kenny Rogers, Jackson Browne, Boz Scaggs, Tom Waits, and countless others. His association with Little Feat dates back to the 1970s, when he collaborated as a sideman on several of their albums, including Dixie Chicken and Time Loves a Hero. He became an official member of the band in 1988 when they released the legendary album Let It Roll. Tackett's multifaceted musicianship and extensive career have solidified his reputation as a distinguished figure in the American music scene. American music courses through Scott Sharrard’s heart and soul. Since 2019, Sharrard has been the lead singer and guitarist for Little Feat, preserving the magic of the group’s beloved guiding light, the late Lowell George, and reimagining that energy with his own mojo. He’s recorded on their Grammy-Nominated album, Sam’s Place. He performed, wrote and co-produced songs for their 2025 album, Strike Up The Band. As a guitarist, he has shared the stage with Derek Trucks, Warren Haynes, Tommy Emmanuel, Robert Randolph, Charlie Starr, and Hubert Sumlin. Prior to Little Feat, Sharrard served as Gregg Allman’s music director, band leader and producer for man...
events.location: Egyptian Theatre, Park City, UT

events.title: Psychedelic Furs with Special Guest Living Colour
events.description: The Psychedelic Furs may not have invented rock & roll per se, but their influence since arriving on the post-punk scorched-earth landscape four decades ago has reverberated and resonated among all those who cherish the sweet-and-sour spot where rawness and romanticism meet. Born out of the UK post-punk scene and led by the vocalist and songwriter Richard Butler, and his bass-wielding brother Tim, the Furs quickly developed as one of the premiere bands at college and alternative radio, scoring a flurry of major hits with "Love My Way," "Pretty In Pink," "Heaven," "The Ghost In You, “And “Heartbreak Beat” in all releasing eight studio albums, spawning several compilations, a boxed set, a live concert DVD, and inspiring one of the most iconic motion picture soundtracks of all time. Decades later, their latest release, “Made Of Rain” charted in a multitude of countries and became the Furs’ Second-highest-ranked UK Album ever. It was prominently featured at the end of the year “Best Albums” chart roundup in dozens of publications worldwide. But the Furs especially thrive live in concert, having headlined at the U.K.’s famed Glastonbury Festival, and more recently at esteemed venues including the Hollywood Bowl, London’s Royal Albert Hall, New York’s Radio City Music Hall,and Nashville’s Grand ‘le Opry, among others. With a dazzling live show, they continue to tour quite regularly across the globe Living Colour Few bands have reshaped the sound and spirit of rock music like Living Colour. Formed in New York City in 1984 and led by visionary guitarist Vernon Reid, the band—Corey Glover \(vocals\), Will Calhoun \(drums\), and Doug Wimbish \(bass\)—delivers a powerful fusion of rock, funk, jazz, and metal that remains as bold and relevant today as ever. Living Colour burst onto the scene with their groundbreaking debut album Vivid \(1988\), which climbed to \#6 on the Billboard 200 and earned double-platinum status. The album featured the iconic anthem “Cult of Perso...

events.title: Reception & Five-Course Dinner
events.description: The Taste of Luxury Series featuring Stag's Leap — Deer Valley’s Taste of Luxury Series continues with a celebration honoring the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Judgment of Paris, the landmark tasting that forever changed the global perception of California wine and established Napa Valley as a world-class wine region. To commemorate the occasion, Deer Valley welcomes Marcus Notaro, head winemaker of Stag's Leap Wine Cellars, alongside returning guest Chef Clement Gelas of Talisker Club for an intimate evening at Deer Valley Café. Set pond-side at sunset, guests will enjoy a thoughtfully curated five-course pairing experience inspired by the wines, stories, and legacy behind this groundbreaking moment in wine history. events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 1 and 2, Chef Ryan Arensdorf of KC Que takes the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Chef Peter Menteer prepares paella in an 8-foot pan with local meats and fresh seafood, alongside Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon cooked over open fire pits. Local Utah breweries and live entertainment at Silver Lake Village round out the experience. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Boney James
events.description: Saxophonist, Producer and Composer Boney James’ New Album ‘Slow Burn’ slow burn: noun, informal A feeling that grows with a slow but deliberate intensity. For saxophonist and composer Boney James, these words perfectly describe the music he has crafted for his new Concord Records album, Slow Burn, to be released on October 18, 2024. “When I mentioned that phrase to people as a possible title, they said, “That also refers to your whole career in a way” James says with a laugh. “This is my 19th record and I feel like I’m just hitting my stride.” Slow Burn is a new high point in a remarkable career that now spans more than three decades and includes a long list of accolades; four GRAMMY nominations, three NAACP Image Award nominations, a Soul Train Award for Best Jazz Album and four RIAA Gold Record certifications. In 2009, Billboard named James one of the Top 3 Contemporary Jazz Artists of the decade. In 2024 year Boney became the first ever artist to score 20 number one singles on the Billboard Smooth Jazz chart. But Boney James has never been one to look backwards and Slow Burn, which the artist produced, marks several firsts. Beginning with the album’s opening track, ‘Arcadia.’ The very first notes on the album are the acoustic bass playing of the legendary Marcus Miller. It’s one of the few times that Miller, known for his electric bass guitar work, has committed his standup bass playing to a recording and the first time that James has incorporated an upright bass into his own music Next is James’ intriguing interpretation of the classic ‘Butterfly.’ Co-written by master keyboardist Herbie Hancock and Bennie Maupin, the track features Marcus Miller again as well as young lion Cory Henry’s inspired electric piano. “This beautiful melody got stuck in my head” explains James. “I have never covered a tune by a legend like Herbie before and I hope we did him justice.” The title track, ‘Slow Burn,’ is one of four tunes on the album that James co-wrote an

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

He became an official member of the band in 1988 when they released the legendary album Let It Roll. Tackett's multifaceted musicianship and extensive career have solidified his reputation as a distinguished figure in the American music scene. American music courses through Scott Sharrard’s heart and soul. Since 2019, Sharrard has been the lead singer and guitarist for Little Feat, preserving the magic of the group’s beloved guiding light, the late Lowell George, and reimagining that energy with his own mojo. He’s recorded on their Grammy-Nominated album, Sam’s Place. He performed, wrote and co-produced songs for their 2025 album, Strike Up The Band. As a guitarist, he has shared the stage with Derek Trucks, Warren Haynes, Tommy Emmanuel, Robert Randolph, Charlie Starr, and Hubert Sumlin. Prior to Little Feat, Sharrard served as Gregg Allman’s music director, band leader and producer for man...

events.title: Haydn, Mendelssohn, & A Viola Concerto
events.description: The rich voice of the viola takes center stage as Utah Symphony Principal Viola Brant Bayless performs Dai Fujikura’s Wayfinder, a concerto inspired by Polynesian navigation traditions. In this imaginative work, the orchestra becomes the natural forces used to find direction—stars, winds, and ocean swells—while the soloist listens and charts a path forward through shifting musical currents. Like a musical guide through the evening, Maurice Cohn conducts a program that unfolds across vivid sonic landscapes, beginning with Mendelssohn’s atmospheric The Hebrides, which evokes Scotland’s rugged coastline with dark, rolling lines in the low strings set against shimmering winds and strings that suggest the sea air. The journey concludes with Haydn’s jubilant Symphony No. 104—his final symphony—whose finale bursts with rustic, folk-inspired energy that resonates beautifully in the intimate acoustics of St. Mary’s Catholic Church....

events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description: Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever. Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid. In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. Light travels through the surface rather than simply reflecting off it, making each piece feel as much like a living object as a painting. Join us August 7th to experience new works by Michael Kessler at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

Enhance the evening with an optional ticket to Chicago the Musical in Concert at the Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater. RESERVE YOUR TICKETS EVENT SCHEDULE Meet-and-Greet Reception 4:30 p.m. Kansas City-Style BBQ Dinner 5 - 6:30 p.m. Chicago the Musical in Concert Doors: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Lonestar
events.description: Lonestar is a celebrated American country music group that earned global recognition with their timeless ballad “Amazed,” a crossover sensation that dominated the charts for 9 weeks at No. 1 on the country charts and 2 weeks at No. 1 on the pop charts. Known for blending heartfelt storytelling with a polished pop-country sound, the band — comprised of Dean Sams, Michael Britt, Keech Rainwater, and Drew Womack — has made a lasting impact on the genre. With an impressive 22 top ten singles on the Billboard Hot Country Songs chart, including 10 that reached No. 1, Lonestar has built one of the most successful track records in modern country music. Their rise began in 1995 with the top-five single “Tequila Talking,” followed by chart-topping hits such as the rock-tinged “No News,” “Come Cryin’ to Me,” and “Everything’s Changed.” The release of their quadruple-platinum 1999 album Lonely Grill firmly established their legacy, producing four No. 1 singles and showcasing the band’s signature ability to craft emotionally resonant, radio-friendly songs. Lonestar’s achievements have earned them widespread acclaim, including multiple Academy of Country Music awards for New Vocal Group, Single of the Year, Song of the Year, and Humanitarian of the Year. They have also been honored by the Country Music Association with Vocal Group of the Year and the International Artist Achievement Award. With more than 10.5 million records sold worldwide, Lonestar remains one of country music’s most enduring and influential bands, continuing to inspire fans with their heartfelt hits and remarkable musical legacy.

events.address: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” where sweet memories offer fleeting comfort after a lover’s betrayal. Famously called “the apotheosis of the dance itself” by Wagner, Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7 brings the night to a triumphant close. An instant symphonic favorite—with early audiences reportedly demanding a repeat of the second movement—the piece surges with wildness and joy, culminating in one of the most electrifying finales in classical music....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Country Weekend - Walker Hayes
events.description: Country superstar Walker Hayes is a singer-songwriter from Mobile, Alabama, known for blending country storytelling with pop and hip-hop-inspired production. His breakout smash “Fancy Like” topped country sales and streaming charts, spent over six months at No. 1 on Billboard’s Hot Country Songs chart, reached the Top 5 on the Billboard Hot 100, hit No. 1 at country radio, and earned Hayes his first GRAMMY nomination for Best Country Song. The viral hit was also featured in a nationwide Applebee’s commercial. With additional fan favorites like “AA,” “Y’all Life,” and “U Gurl,” Hayes has become one of the top-selling and most-streamed artists in modern country music. His latest project, 17 Problems, is his most personal work to date—an introspective album shaped by resilience, faith, family, grief, and growth. Featuring both high-energy anthems and deeply emotional storytelling, the album highlights Hayes’ evolution as an artist and songwriter. Known for his authenticity and engaging live performances, Hayes has appeared on Good Morning America, The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, Late Night with Seth Meyers, TODAY, and CBS Sunday Morning. Whether delivering chart-topping hits or stripped-down acoustic moments, Walker Hayes invites audiences into a powerful, honest, and unforgettable live experience.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT
events.address: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Live Music at Silver Lake
events.description: Food Trucks, Music & Scenic Adventures — Join us Friday through Sunday on Silver Lake Lawn for live music, local food trucks, and relaxed mountain afternoons. Settle into a lounge chair, enjoy a cold drink, and take in alpine views while a rotating lineup of artists fills the air with acoustic rock, country, blues, and more. With hiking trails just steps away, it’s an easy, all-day experience for everyone. BUY LIFT TICKETS THIS WEEKEND’S LINEUP Friday, August 7 Cry Wolf Saturday, August 8 Pompe n Honey Sunday, August 9 Vorraro
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Idina Menzel
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Calling all Broadway and musical theater lovers to Park City! Tony Award®-winning, powerhouse vocalist Idina Menzel joins forces with the Utah Symphony for a one-night-only performance featuring songs from her defining roles in Rent, Wicked, and Disney’s Frozen. Be there for music from across her career and your favorites like “Defying Gravity,” “Let it Go,” and “Take Me or Leave Me,” as the orchestra joins her soaring voice and takes the melodies you know by heart to new heights. This Signature performance is not eligible for any discounts. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA
events.venue_name: Deer Valley Ski Resort Main Base

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 8 and 9, Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to take the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Chef Peter Menteer prepares paella in an 8-foot pan with local meats and fresh seafood, alongside Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon cooked over open fire pits. Local Utah breweries and live entertainment at Silver Lake Village round out the experience. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Concerts on the Slopes
events.description: Walker Hayes returns with his most introspective and heartfelt project yet, 17 Problems, an album shaped by reflection, resilience, and raw honesty. Blending hip-hop-infused production with country storytelling, Hayes dives deep into themes of fatherhood, faith, grief, and growth. From the aching vulnerability of “17 Year Old Problems” and “Song for My Son” to the high-energy anthems “Mother” and “Neon Lights,” the album captures the full spectrum of life’s beauty and messiness. With over 100 songs written for this release, Hayes carefully curated 17 tracks that reveal the most personal side of his artistry to date, working with top collaborators like Warren “Oak” Felder, Shane McAnally, and Bart Millard while also penning deeply personal solo material. To bring this story to life, Hayes has announced the Walker Hayes Unplugged Tour, where he offers fans an intimate, acoustic experience unlike anything he’s done before. Stripping back the production to let the lyrics lead, Hayes takes audiences on a journey through the highs and lows that shaped him - from viral fame to quiet family moments, from deep heartbreak to enduring faith. Through heartfelt storytelling and soul-stirring songs, this special evening invites fans into the heart of his story. More than a concert, it’s a celebration of connection, resilience, and the messy, beautiful reality of life, one song at a time....
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: UB40 featuring Ali Campbell
events.description: UB40 Featuring Ali Campbell – Live in Concert Get ready for a legendary night of reggae under the stars as UB40 featuring Ali Campbell brings their iconic sound to Concerts on the Slopes in Park City, where Music Meets the Mountains. With Ali Campbell’s unmistakable voice leading the way, UB40 has delivered some of the most beloved songs of all time, including Red Red Wine, Can’t Help Falling in Love, Kingston Town, Here I Am (Come and Take Me), and Food for Thought. Their music has defined generations, blending reggae rhythms with pop sensibility and global appeal. Set against the stunning backdrop of Canyons Village at Park City Mountain, Concerts on the Slopes offers a one-of-a-kind outdoor concert experience. Enjoy cool mountain temperatures, sweeping alpine views, and an energetic yet intimate setting with 5,000 seats and top-tier sound and production. Make it a full Park City experience—arrive early, explore the village, and settle in for an unforgettable summer evening filled with timeless music, mountain air, and good vibes. VIP1) “Kingston Town Front-Row” Experience Includes: Premium Ticket in Front Standing Pit Area Meet & greet photo with Ali Campbell Signed lyric sheet Exclusive VIP merch bundle (e.g., a themed tote, candle, or limited-edition poster) VIP laminate & lanyard Early entry / dedicated VIP entrance Crowd-free merchandise zone (VIP2) "Many Rivers" VIP Merch Bundle Includes: Premium Reserved Seat Exclusive VIP merch bundle (e.g., a themed tote, candle, or limited-edition poster) VIP laminate & lanyard Early entry / dedicated VIP entrance Signed lyric sheet Crowd-free merchandise zone

events.title: Better Than Ezra with Special Guests Vertical Horizon and TONIC
events.description: Get ready for a night of iconic ’90s and early-2000s alternative rock as Better Than Ezra and Vertical Horizon take the stage together for an unforgettable live show. Better Than Ezra, known for their chart-topping hits like “Good,” “Desperately Wanting,” and “King of New Orleans,” rose to prominence during the alternative rock boom of the 1990s. With their melodic hooks, thoughtful lyrics, and energetic live performances, the band has remained a fan favorite for decades. Joining them is Vertical Horizon, the multi-platinum band behind massive radio hits “Everything You Want,” “You’re a God,” and “Best I Ever Had.” Their emotionally driven songwriting and polished rock sound helped define an era of modern alternative and continue to resonate with audiences today. Together, these two beloved bands deliver a hit-packed night of nostalgia, sing-along anthems, and timeless alternative rock. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this show promises a high-energy, feel-good concert experience you won’t want to miss. Music has an undeniable way of bringing people together, creating connections that run as deep as family. That bond is at the heart of Tonic, the GRAMMY® Award–nominated, multi-platinum rock trio made up of Emerson Hart (vocals, guitar), Jeff Russo (lead guitar), and Dan Lavery (bass). Since forming in the mid-1990s, Tonic has built a lasting legacy through eloquent songwriting, lyrical honesty, and unforgettable melodies. Their debut album Lemon Parade went platinum and produced the iconic “If You Could Only See,” the #1 most-played rock song of 1997. The band went on to score six Top 10 singles, sell over 4 million records, and earn two GRAMMY® Award nominations, including for “Take Me As I Am.” More than two decades later, Tonic’s music continues to resonate across generations. Fans—young and old—still sing every word, proving the songs are bigger than time and bigger than the band itself. After brief breaks and successful solo caree

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Maoli
events.description: Now breaking into the mainstream with a one-of-a-kind blend of island reggae and country soul, Hawaiian-born rising star Maoli is bringing his people—and his sound—to the world like never before. The golden-voiced native of upcountry Maui has released nine independent albums, earned over half a billion global streams, and spent nearly 15 years touring and recording, becoming a major fixture across the Pacific Islands and beyond. Inspired by traditional Hawaiian music (the birthplace of the steel guitar), reggae, rock, and country legends like Dolly Parton, Willie Nelson, and George Strait, Maoli has built a signature style that connects Polynesia to the American West Coast—and all the way to Texas. With a growing global fanbase, Maoli’s Hawaiian-country sound has fueled viral hits and powerful covers, including Brett Young’s “Mercy” (18+ million Spotify streams) and “In Case You Didn’t Know” (10+ million Spotify streams). Now teaming up with hit producers Dann and David Huff, Maoli continues bridging the gap between island soul and country tradition with his latest release, Last Sip of Summer (Island Vibe Edition)—out now.

events.title: Cory Mon
events.description: ..music, my blessing, my curse, its in me and it must come out i can't help it, i must play, write, compose and perform. Without i am incomplete, while with call me misfit in this world of the incorporated. I am a man confused and divided thus in recourse i am often brightened and enlightened. I emphatically give way to both light and dark, i live in pleasure and pain. my name: cory. Singer/songwriter Cory Mon has a distinctly unique, albeit pleasant voice in the world of music. “Happy \(sometimes sad\), gritty, funky, folksy, bluesy, rocky music… I guess”… he says when asked the dreaded, “So what type of music do you play?” Assuming the life of a musician, Cory has thrived as well as endured through the many ups and downs that have come throughout his well tenured 20\+ year music career. Cory has released 60\+ original songs and toured with and/or supported a handful of his favorite artists: JJ Grey & Mofro, Sean Hayes, The Avett Brothers, Patterson Hood etc. www.corymon.com...

events.description: You've read the play - maybe seen the movie, the musical, e ven the ballet. events.description: **Four-part WANDER series focused on women’s hormonal health throughout the seasons (with 8 bonus monthly Zoom calls for monthly check ins)** ***Are fatigue, mood shifts, or hormonal changes dimming your spark?*** You’re not alone. Hormones shape every part of our lives—and they’re influenced by the seasons, both in nature and within ourselves. Whether you’re navigating postpartum recovery, fertility planning, perimenopause, postmenopause, or simply wanting more energy and clarity, this series will help you realign with your body’s wisdom and the earth’s cycles. Step into the unique rhythm of each season with Wild Women Tribe’s **Seasonal Hormone Series**, co-hosted by naturopathic physician Dr. Trevor Cates, in a powerful blend of nature, wellness and community. This transformative quarterly series invites you to connect deeply with nature’s cycles through intentional movement, seasonal nourishment, and a sisterhood community of other women who are committed to their natural health journey. DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/wander-1 SIGN UP: wildwomentribe.net/elemental-harmony-registration **Seasonal WANDER Sessions (In-Person in Park City + Virtual Option):** Each seasonal IN PERSON gathering includes: **Guided mindful hike** + elemental ritual **Movement & meditation practices** themed to the season **Seasonal hormone workshop** with Dr. Trevor Cates **Community connection circle** **Quarterly group challenge** SESSION THEMES: **Fall (Nov. 15): Grounding & Balance // Earth** **Winter (Jan. 3): Reflection & Restoration // Water** **Spring (Apr. 18): Renewal & Reawakening // Air** **Summer (Aug. 22): Vibrancy & Vitality // Fire** Each quarterly gathering unfolds with an outdoor mindful hike through Park City’s scenic landscapes, paired with movement and mindset practices aligned with each season. Each session offers practical insights on aligning your **nutrition, supplements, mindset, and movement** with your body’s changing needs — modeled after...

events.title: Rick Springfield
events.description: RICK SPRINGFIELD BIO: Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” "I've Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years. Rick Springfield Premium Ticket & Merch Package . Premium Concert Ticket (or GA where applicable). Exclusive T-Shirt . events.title: Dylan Scott
events.description: ABOUT DYLAN SCOTT:Despite a multi-Platinum story and six No. 1 singles, including two fresh smashes in 2025 alone, Curb Records hitmaker Dylan Scott has always kept an underdog mindset. A rowdy roughneck with a romantic side – one whose modern edge was sharpened by timeless, emotional twang – the consummate family man broke out with the epic triple-Platinum devotion of “My Girl” in 2016 and followed up with scrappy, under-the-radar consistency. Chart-topping heart-forward anthems like “Hooked,” “Nobody” and “New Truck” all reached RIAA-certified status, but last year launched the crooner into the stratosphere. Posting back-to-back No. 1s with Gold-certified “Boys Back Home” (with Dylan Marlowe) and Platinum-certified “This Town’s Been Too Good to Us,” Scott wrapped 2025 as the third most-played country artist of 2025 and continues his hot streak with his current single, Platinum-certified “What He’ll Never Have,” which nears the Top 10 at Country radio and was honored by ASCAP as one of the most-performed country songs of 2025. With nearly 5 billion career streams, legions of fans and countless sold-out shows to his credit, his stardom has somehow grown in relative stealth. But with his third full album, Easy Does It, he’s not sweating. Sure of himself, his talent and his fans, Scott heads toward a new creative chapter with total confidence. Featuring 11 tracks, his signature vocal and vivid intensity take center stage on a set defined by personal truth – whether through heartbreak, romantic devotion or small-town dedication. The in-demand live act spent last year on his Country Till I Die Tour, headlining his biggest venues yet including his first-ever arenas and record-breaking attendance, and his Easy Does It Tour. He will continue headlining across the country on his Till I Can’t I Will Tour, launching this spring.

events.title: Eastern Dreamers
events.description: Two young musicians from Nashville, TN. Max Pachter and Evan deNicola of the "Eastern Dreamers" bring 70s Folk and Americana to life with their unique guitar-based approach to their performances and songwriting. Max's raw and smoky voice paired with Evan's pure and heady tenor create a contrasting, but soothing dynamic when harmonizing together. Paying homage in their sound to the likes of "Simon & Garfunkel" and "Crosby, Stills, and Nash", their authentic, heartfelt storytelling along with their clear musical abilities create a deeply awe-inspiring and intimate experience for their listeners.
events.location: The Spur Bar and Grill, Park City, UT

events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description: Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him. Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next. The result is painting that moves the way wilderness does, familiar and elusive at once, inviting you closer while always suggesting there's something just beyond the next ridge. Join us September 5th to experience new works by Matt Flint at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Fox US Open of Mountain Biking
events.description: The Official US National Mountain Bike Championship — The Fox US Open of Mountain Biking is coming to Deer Valley! Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Pro Finals will take place on Saturday, September 12. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.

events.title: Moose on the Loose Kids Trail Race
events.description: Join us for **Moose on the Loose 2026,** a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are **June 13**, **July 18**, **August 15**, and **September 12**, with all races beginning at **9:00 AM**. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....

events.title: ACE men's group
events.description: ACE men's group Thursday, September 17th | 5:30 PM | Alta Community Center | Free The ACE Men's Group purpose is to provide personal and professional support to men connected to the Alta and Little Cottonwood communities. The power in groups lies in the unique opportunity to give and receive multiple perspectives, support, encouragement and feedback to and from other individuals in a safe and confidential environment. The goal and hope is that you will benefit from your experience here. Daniel is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker practicing in Salt Lake City. His practice focuses on supporting individuals to develop insight into themselves, plan for making positive change and execution of those plans to reach their goals. Daniel is an lover of the outdoors, especially Alta and all it has to offer. Following meetings of each month: 6/18 7/16 8/20 9/17 10/15 ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all our attendees. Please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 national Day of Service and Remembrance
events.description: Save the Date for Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 National Day of Service and Remembrance. The day starts at 8 a.m. in City Park with a complimentary breakfast for all volunteers, followed by a short program that honors 9/11/01. At 9 a.m. all volunteers head to their Serve Park City service projects they have signed up for on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity that are being held on 9/19/26. (These projects will be posted by August 1, 2026) Last year we had 35 different projects all over the Park City area that ran from 9 a.m. -12 noon with over 1200 volunteers participating. We invite all to serve. This event is hosted by Serve Park City, Inc. A nonprofit whose mission is to build community through service. Each year we partner with over 30 nonprofits and organizations in hosting a day of service, but find opportunities to serve throughout the year on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity. Learn more about us at serveparkcity.org...

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: ACE men's group
events.description: ACE men's group Thursday, October 15th | 5:30 PM | Alta Community Center | Free The ACE Men's Group purpose is to provide personal and professional support to men connected to the Alta and Little Cottonwood communities. The power in groups lies in the unique opportunity to give and receive multiple perspectives, support, encouragement and feedback to and from other individuals in a safe and confidential environment. The goal and hope is that you will benefit from your experience here. Daniel is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker practicing in Salt Lake City. His practice focuses on supporting individuals to develop insight into themselves, plan for making positive change and execution of those plans to reach their goals. Daniel is an lover of the outdoors, especially Alta and all it has to offer. Following meetings of each month: 6/18 7/16 8/20 9/17 10/15 ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all our attendees. Please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, October 22nd | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Cottonwood Canyon. Christopher Merritt received his Ph.D. from the University of Montana in Anthropology in 2010, focusing on archaeological and historical investigations of the Overseas Chinese. Before that, he received a Master's of Science in Industrial Archaeology from Michigan Technological University, where he studied the trade and consumption of Mormon-produced pottery in Utah. Since 2004, Merritt has worked in a variety of professional archaeological settings, including the United States Forest Service in Utah and Nevada, private archaeological contracting in Salt Lake City, and, finally, as the State Historic Preservation Officer for the Utah Division of State History. In addition to the full-time employment with the State of Utah, he is Adjunct Faculty in Anthropology at Salt Lake Community College and an instructor for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.description: Hosted by the Park City Gallery Association, the Last Friday Gallery Stroll, is a fun \(and free\) monthly community event dedicated to highlighting artists and special exhibits at art galleries throughout town. Park City's art scene continues to thrive, offering a robust variety of unique art collections from local, national, and international artists. On the last Friday of each month, seventeen of our art galleries open their doors for visitors and locals alike to enjoy light refreshments and to admire their latest art installations. Park City Gallery Stroll runs from 6:00 - 9:00pm on the last Friday of each month. It's the perfect way to explore the Park City art scene while enjoying scenic Historic Main Street and our beautiful alpine evenings....

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 8 and 9, Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to take the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Chef Peter Menteer prepares paella in an 8-foot pan with local meats and fresh seafood, alongside Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon cooked over open fire pits. Local Utah breweries and live entertainment at Silver Lake Village round out the experience. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Roméo et Juliette
events.description: You've read the play - maybe seen the movie, the musical, e ven the ballet. But have you experienced the opera?August 21 at 7:00 PM & August 23 at 3:00 PM Eccles Center, Park City In Charles Gounod's Roméo et Juliette, Shakespeare's star-crossed lovers come to life amid the pageantry and passion of Verona, reimagined through the grand orchestral and theatrical language of French Romantic opera. Arrive early to explore displays of art, history, and themed concessions that set the scene for Roméo et Juliette, and stay after for a Q&A with the artists and creative team. A 40-player orchestra and soaring voices deliver the full grandeur of opera in an immersive experience close to home....
events.location: Eccles Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: Fox US Open of Mountain Biking
events.description: The Official US National Mountain Bike Championship — The Fox US Open of Mountain Biking is coming to Deer Valley! Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Pro Finals will take place on Saturday, September 12. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.
events.location: Deer Valley Resort, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." GorgeousGourds.net...
events.location: Park Silly Sunday Market, Park City, UT

events.title: Eric Heideman
events.description: Eric Heideman is a 27-year-old blues musician living in Austin, Texas, from Salt Lake City, Utah. Heideman was personally requested to open shows for ZZ Top at Eccles Theatre and Walter Trout and Mike Zito at The State Room. He was a featured performer at the Utah Blues Fest in 2022, 2023, & 2024. He was a featured performer at SXSW 2025 and the 2025 Durango Blues Train. He recorded his latest album, “Third Degree Gravity” with Grammy Nominated Victor Wainwright and International Blues Challenge winner JW-Jones.

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: yoga in the mountains

This event is free and no tickets are required.

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: fearless abstract painting
events.description: fearless abstract painting Tuesday , August 18th | 6PM | Alta Peruvian Lawn | $15 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to create fearless, intuitive abstract paintings using Samantha da Silva's signature "no rules" roller-painting method, incorporating plaster, salt, sand, sawdust, and playful canvas manipulation techniques. This class is especially good for people who want to learn how to silence their inner critic, overcome fear of the blank canvas, trust their intuition, and discover their own authentic artistic voice in a joyful, judgment-free space. You will use rollers \(instead of brushes\), large quantities of acrylic paint and water, plaster, salt, sand, sawdust, and your hands directly on the canvas laid flat on a table. At the end of the class, you will leave with one completed, textured abstract painting, a toolbox of fearless techniques you can use forever, and the confidence to keep creating without rules or self-doubt. Samantha da Silva \(b. 1978, Brazil\) is a Brazilian-born, Salt Lake City-based artist and dedicated educator who has taught visual arts for over twenty years in public and private schools and institutions across Canada, the United States, Hawaii, and France. Shaped by nearly forty moves triggered by natural disasters and life circumstances, and by the intense assimilation she experienced at age ten, da Silva channels themes of migration, belonging, and resourcefulness into her widely recognized large-scale relief sculptures-topographic-like works built from found dirt, sawdust, and debris-that have appeared in Architectural Digest, Bravo TV, and HGTV. In the classroom, she guides students of all ages to transform personal experience and everyday materials into powerful expression, making art a vital tool for connection and resilience in an ever-shifting world. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions...

events.description: Join us at Snowbird for Military Appreciation Week. Active and retired Military and their immediate family will receive a FREE All-Day Summer Scenic Tram Ride pass to the top of Hidden Peak. How to Redeem Your Tickets: 1. Visit http://bit.ly/4n7RLQu, exclusively for Military members. Select the date and number of additional passes you would like to redeem. Add them to your Snowbird cart and checkout. 2. Assign your tickets and select whether each person needs a ticket card. 3. Fill out the Sheer ID verification with your information \(https://services.sheerid.com/verify/6407ade40bd23a3ee0613db1/\). 4. Once your military status is verified, input the code you receive into the box on the checkout page. 5. Finish the checkout process. events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, January 14th | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated, and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones, and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Cottonwood Canyon. Christopher Merritt received his Ph.D. from the University of Montana in Anthropology in 2010, focusing on archaeological and historical investigations of the Overseas Chinese. Before that, he received a Master's of Science in Industrial Archaeology from Michigan Technological University, where he studied the trade and consumption of Mormon-produced pottery in Utah. Since 2004, Merritt has worked in a variety of professional archaeological settings, including the United States Forest Service in Utah and Nevada, private archaeological contracting in Salt Lake City, and, finally, as the State Historic Preservation Officer for the Utah Division of State History. In addition to the full-time employment with the State of Utah, he is Adjunct Faculty in Anthropology at Salt Lake Community College and an instructor for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center
